title: Intraday Normalized Item
description: Source item after normalization. Canonical internal format used by all downstream stages: deduplication, clustering, AI classification, alert creation, and summary generation. Every source type (RSS news, official RSS, data API, research RSS, X/social) maps into this structure.

item_id.description: Deterministic identifier: SHA-256( source_name + ':' + source_id ), hex-encoded. Used for deduplication.

source_id.description: Original source identifier, preserved from the source item.

source_slug.description: Stable registry identifier for the source (e.g. 'coindesk-rss'). Propagated from the source item. Null when the source is not registered in the D1 sources table.

source_type.description: Technical source category. Determines parser path in module 01 and informs trust-tier logic in module 05/06.

source_url.description: Canonical URL of the original item.

headline.description: Cleaned, truncated headline. HTML stripped. Max 250 characters.

body.description: Cleaned body text. HTML stripped. Max 2000 characters. Null when not available.

author.description: Author name, journalist byline, or X account handle (@username) when available from the source. Null when not provided. Passed to AI classification for attribution and trust context.

topic_candidates.description: Topic slugs identified by lightweight keyword matching before the AI step. Empty array when no keyword match is found; the AI classifier will still assign a topic.

published_at.description: Best-available event timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC). Falls back to fetched_at when source omits it.

fetched_at.description: UTC timestamp when the item was fetched.

trust_tier.description: Trust classification propagated from the D1 sources registry. T1=Official, T2=Wire, T3=Specialist, T4=Signal/Social. Null when the source is not registered. Used by module 05 (AI classification) to adjust confidence_score and by module 06 (alert decision) to enforce per-topic severity caps.

trust_score.description: Numeric trust score (0–100) from the D1 sources registry. Higher is more trusted. Null when the source is not registered. Passed to AI classification for confidence calibration.

is_duplicate.description: Set to true by the deduplication stage (module 03). Items where is_duplicate=true are dropped before AI classification.

examples.headline: Bitcoin Hits New All-Time High Above $120K
examples.body: Bitcoin surged past $120,000 on Wednesday, setting a new all-time high as institutional demand continued to rise.
examples.author: Omkar Godbole

examples.headline: Consumer Price Index — December 2024
examples.body: The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers rose 0.3 percent in December on a seasonally adjusted basis.

examples.headline: Scaling Laws for Next-Token Prediction Beyond 1T Parameters
examples.body: We present empirical scaling laws for autoregressive language models trained at scales beyond 1 trillion parameters.
examples.author: Jane Smith, John Doe, et al.

examples.headline: 🚨 1,500 #BTC (183,200,000 USD) transferred from unknown wallet to #Binance
examples.author: @whale_alert
